---
title: Reference template
description: A lookup-oriented layout for APIs, configuration keys, commands, and syntax.
---

Use this layout when readers need facts on demand — not a story or a walkthrough. Reference pages are for scanning, copying exact syntax, and confirming allowed values.

<Info>
  Examples on the **Templates** pages share a fictional **Coffee Shop Ordering API** so you can compare how the same product reads across documentation types.
</Info>

## When to use it

Pick the reference layout for surfaces where accuracy and completeness matter more than narrative.

| Use reference when… | Use something else when… |
| --- | --- |
| The reader knows *what* they need to look up | They need a first success path → [Tutorial](/templates/tutorial) |
| Fields, types, defaults, and errors must be exhaustive | They need steps for one task → [How-to](/templates/how-to) |
| Prose should stay minimal | They need architecture and tradeoffs → [Explanation](/templates/explanation) |

## The goal

Provide a strict, lookup-oriented directory of facts: API specs, configuration keys, command syntax, or component props.

## The tone

Stark, literal, objective, and dense. No welcome paragraphs, analogies, or marketing language.

## Recommended layout

Structure every reference page with these sections:

1. **Syntax or blueprint** — The exact format: endpoint, command, or file shape.
2. **Parameters or options** — Tables or `<Property>` blocks for fields, types, defaults, and constraints.
3. **Examples** — Valid snippets only; link out for walkthroughs.
4. **Errors** — Status codes or failure conditions when applicable.
5. **See also** — Links to how-to and explanation pages.

## Copy this template

Copy the skeleton below into a new `.mdx` file and replace placeholder paths and content with your own.

````mdx title="docs/reference/create-order.mdx"
---
title: Create order
description: Request parameters, examples, and errors for POST /v1/orders.
---

Create a new beverage order.

## Syntax

```http
POST /v1/orders
Authorization: Bearer <api_key>
Content-Type: application/json
```

## Request parameters

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `item` | `string` | Yes | Base beverage. Allowed values: `espresso`, `americano`, `latte`, `cappuccino`. |
| `size` | `string` | Yes | Drink size. Allowed values: `single`, `double`, `twelve_oz`, `sixteen_oz`. |
| `temperature` | `string` | No | Defaults to `hot`. Allowed values: `hot`, `iced`. |

## Example request

```json
{
  "item": "latte",
  "size": "twelve_oz",
  "temperature": "iced"
}
```

## Example response

```json
{
  "order_id": "ord_123",
  "status": "received",
  "total_cents": 525
}
```

## Errors

| Status | Condition |
| --- | --- |
| `401 Unauthorized` | Missing or invalid API key. |
| `422 Unprocessable Entity` | Invalid combination — for example, `single` size with an `iced` latte. |

## See also

- [Create your first coffee order](/path/to/tutorial)
- [Customize drinks with milk alternatives](/path/to/how-to)
- [Order fulfillment lifecycle](/path/to/explanation)
````

## Example: Create order (`POST /v1/orders`)

Below is how a reference page might read for our fictional coffee API. Notice the absence of onboarding prose and the emphasis on allowed values.

### Syntax

```http
POST /v1/orders
Authorization: Bearer <api_key>
Content-Type: application/json
```

### Request parameters

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `item` | `string` | Yes | Base beverage. Allowed values: `espresso`, `americano`, `latte`, `cappuccino`. |
| `size` | `string` | Yes | Drink size. Allowed values: `single`, `double`, `twelve_oz`, `sixteen_oz`. |
| `temperature` | `string` | No | Defaults to `hot`. Allowed values: `hot`, `iced`. |

### Example request

```json
{
  "item": "latte",
  "size": "twelve_oz",
  "temperature": "iced"
}
```

### Example response

```json
{
  "order_id": "ord_123",
  "status": "received",
  "total_cents": 525
}
```

### Errors

| Status | Condition |
| --- | --- |
| `401 Unauthorized` | Missing or invalid API key. |
| `422 Unprocessable Entity` | Invalid combination — for example, `single` size with an `iced` latte. |

## How reference treats code

In reference, code blocks are **data definitions** — request shapes, config snippets, and response samples. Do not embed numbered procedures; link to [Tutorial](/templates/tutorial) or [How-to](/templates/how-to) instead.

Use `<Property>` and `<Accordion>` when you have nested or grouped fields. Use tables when a flat parameter list is enough.
